name: Peñablanca Loop from Vidángoz
name_original: Vuelta al Peñablanca – circular desde Vidángoz por Roncal
description: Tackle the difficult 22.8-mile Vuelta al Peñablanca mountain biking route in Roncal Valley, featuring 4044 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Vidángoz/Bidankoze offers a diverse landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, mountain passes, and forested valleys within the Pyrenean region. The mountainous terrain, carved by the Biniés ravine and its tributaries, provides a varied backdrop for cycling. Riders can expect routes that traverse varied natural environments, including open areas and wooded sections. The region's infrastructure includes dedicated mountain bike trails catering to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Vidángoz/Bidankoze The most popular mountain bike trail is Peñablanca Loop from Vidángoz, a 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cueva del Ibón – Pierre Saint-Martin Pass loop from Isaba / Izaba, a difficult 32.9 miles (52.9 km) path. This route navigates through mountain passes and valleys, offering views of the surrounding natural environment. Local mountain bikers also love the Isaba – Isaba Village loop from Isaba / Izaba, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours. Mountain biking around Vidángoz/Bidankoze is defined by challenging mountain passes, forested areas, and varied elevation profiles. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Vidángoz/Bidankoze are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Vidángoz/Bidankoze's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Vidángoz/Bidankoze?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails in the Vidángoz/Bidankoze area. These routes primarily feature moderate to difficult terrain, offering diverse challenges for riders.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails for beginners in Vidángoz/Bidankoze?
A: The mountain bike trails around Vidángoz/Bidankoze are generally characterized by significant elevation changes and challenging terrain. There are no routes classified as easy; the trails are either moderate or difficult, making them more suitable for riders with some experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Vidángoz/Bidankoze?
A: You can expect a diverse landscape with substantial elevation changes, mountain passes, and forested valleys. The terrain provides challenging ascents and descents, typical of a mountainous region, traversing varied natural environments including open areas and wooded sections.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Vidángoz/Bidankoze?
A: Yes, many of the trails in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15006284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isaba – Cueva del Ibón loop from Isaba / Izaba&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 30.3 km route, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31854567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Ibón – Pierre Saint-Martin Pass loop from Isaba / Izaba&lt;/a&gt; covers 52.9 km.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see while mountain biking in Vidángoz/Bidankoze?
A: The region offers several natural highlights. You could encounter the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/876139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belabarzeko Urjausia - natural swimming pool&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4926965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ripalda Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5226633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Burgui&lt;/a&gt; gorge is also a significant natural monument in the vicinity.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass through villages or settlements?
A: Yes, some routes offer opportunities to pass through charming local settlements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31856221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isaba – Isaba Village loop from Isaba / Izaba&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 14.6 km route that takes you through Isaba Village itself. Other nearby villages include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5310547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgui&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ochagavía&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Vidángoz/Bidankoze?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the challenging mountain passes, varied elevation profiles, and the natural beauty of the forested areas and valleys.

Q: Are there any routes that feature mountain passes?
A: Yes, mountain passes are a defining feature of the region's mountain biking. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15006349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isaba – Laza Pass (1,129 m) loop from Isaba / Izaba&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 46.0 km route that specifically includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/941372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laza Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in the area?
A: One of the longest routes is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31854567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Ibón – Pierre Saint-Martin Pass loop from Isaba / Izaba&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 52.9 km and typically takes around 4 hours and 3 minutes to complete.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Vidángoz/Bidankoze suitable for families?
A: Given that all trails are classified as either moderate or difficult, with no easy options, the routes around Vidángoz/Bidankoze are generally more suited for experienced mountain bikers rather than families with young children or beginners.

Q: What is the most challenging mountain bike route in Vidángoz/Bidankoze?
A: Many routes offer significant challenges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988531709/penablanca-loop-from-vidangoz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñablanca Loop from Vidángoz&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 36.7 km trail with substantial elevation gain, making it a demanding option for experienced riders.
tips: Beautiful views from the hill where several paths fork that lead to Isaba, Roncal or Vidángoz. A few meters from here is the difficult-to-access peak of Peñablanca.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Vuelta al Peñablanca mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult and demands a very good physical condition and significant mountain biking experience. It features substantial ascents and high mountain terrain over its 36.7-kilometer distance.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Vuelta al Peñablanca mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: On average, it takes about 4 hours and 11 minutes to complete this circular mountain bike route, though this can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vuelta al Peñablanca trail in Vidángoz?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Vidángoz. All BTT circuits in the Roncal Valley are signposted and have information panels at their start points, which usually indicate parking availability. Look for designated parking areas within or near Vidángoz village.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Vuelta al Peñablanca?
faqs.answer: Given its high mountain scenery, the best time to ride this trail is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October), when the weather is more stable and there's less chance of snow or ice at higher elevations. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Vuelta al Peñablanca trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the Roncal Valley are dog-friendly, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a challenging, long ride and that you carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking in the Arabarko BGL / Arrigorrietako Mendilerroa eta Ezkaurre Mendia BGL?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails within the Arabarko BGL / Arrigorrietako Mendilerroa eta Ezkaurre Mendia BGL. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Vuelta al Peñablanca route?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied and potentially technical terrain. The Roncal Valley is known for high mountain landscapes, karst formations, and dense forests. While specific surface details aren't provided, difficult MTB trails in Spain can include gravel roads, singletrack, rocky sections, and steep gradients, sometimes requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers immersive high mountain scenery throughout the Roncal Valley. A notable viewpoint along the way is the Barangada Viewpoint, providing stunning vistas of the surrounding landscapes, including the flowing Esca River and diverse forests.

faqs.question: Does the Vuelta al Peñablanca route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several official trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Senda Pirenaica - E32, E1. Errege bidea: Burgi - Izaba, Via Sancti Martini - Chemin de Saragosse, and various segments of the Carretil de Uztárroz.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in Vidángoz?
faqs.answer: Vidángoz is a small village in the Roncal Valley. While it may have limited options, you can typically find local cafes, pubs, and accommodation in Vidángoz or nearby larger villages in the Roncal Valley to refuel and rest after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for mountain biking this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate safety gear (helmet, gloves), navigation tools (like a GPS device or the komoot app), and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather.
